sent2: Achei o filme muito legal, você tem que ver.
sent2_en: I thought the movie was really great, you have to see it.
cuidado: ⚠️ NO es 'legal' (lícito, conforme a la ley). En portugués coloquial significa 'genial/guay'.

sent1: Valeu por me ajudar com a mudança, hein.
sent1_en: Thanks for helping me with the move, really.
sent2: Beleza, te ligo mais tarde. Valeu!
sent2_en: Alright, I'll call you later. Cheers!
notes: Informal 'thanks' or a casual sign-off, like 'cheers'; used constantly among friends.

sent1: Não tenho grana pra sair esse fim de semana.
sent1_en: I don't have money to go out this weekend.
sent2: Ele ganhou uma grana boa nesse trampo.
sent2_en: He made good money on that job.
notes: Slang for money, very common in spoken Portuguese.

sent1: Vamos dar um rolê no shopping mais tarde?
sent1_en: Want to go hang out at the mall later?
sent2: O rolê de ontem foi muito bom, todo mundo curtiu.
sent2_en: Yesterday's outing was really fun, everyone enjoyed it.
notes: Very common slang among young people for going out or a casual plan.

sent1: Que bagulho é esse em cima da mesa?
sent1_en: What's that thing on top of the table?
sent2: Deixei meu bagulho todo largado no quarto do meu amigo.
sent2_en: I left all my stuff scattered in my friend's room.
notes: Vague word for 'thing/stuff'; in some contexts can slangily mean drugs.

sent1: Não quero treta com ninguém, só vim curtir a festa.
sent1_en: I don't want any trouble with anyone, I just came to enjoy the party.
sent2: Rolou uma treta feia entre os dois lá no bar.
sent2_en: There was some ugly trouble between the two of them at the bar.
notes: Slang for conflict, drama, or a sketchy situation.

sent1: E aí, mano, tudo certo com você?
sent1_en: Hey bro, everything good with you?
sent2: Mano, essa prova foi difícil demais.
sent2_en: Dude, that test was way too hard.
cuidado: ⚠️ NO es 'mano' (la mano, parte del cuerpo). Aquí es una forma de tratamiento informal como 'tío' o 'colega'.

sent1: E aí, galera, bora combinar o que vamos fazer?
sent1_en: So, folks, let's figure out what we're gonna do?
sent2: A galera toda foi pra praia no feriado.
sent2_en: Everyone in the group went to the beach on the holiday.
cuidado: ⚠️ NO es 'galera' (nave/embarcación o celda de prisión). En portugués significa 'la gente/el grupo'.

sent1: Esse moleque não para quieto nem um minuto.
sent1_en: This kid doesn't sit still for even a minute.
sent2: Quando eu era moleque, adorava jogar bola na rua.
sent2_en: When I was a kid, I loved playing ball in the street.
notes: Informal word for a boy/kid, sometimes with a playful or mischievous connotation.

sent1: O garoto tava brincando na rua até de noite.
sent1_en: The kid was playing in the street until nighttime.
sent2: Esse garoto é muito esperto pra idade dele.
sent2_en: This kid is very smart for his age.
notes: Also 'garota' for girl; extremely common everyday word.

sent1: Cadê meu celular, alguém viu?
sent1_en: Where's my phone, has anyone seen it?
sent2: Cadê a Marina? Ela ia chegar cedo.
sent2_en: Where's Marina? She was supposed to arrive early.
notes: Fixed colloquial contraction (from 'que é de'); always used with a noun, never conjugated.
ear: stress falls on the last syllable: ca-DÊ, not CÁ-de

sent1: Já vou embora, tá ficando tarde.
sent1_en: I'm going now, it's getting late.
sent2: Ela foi embora sem se despedir de ninguém.
sent2_en: She left without saying goodbye to anyone.
notes: 'ir embora' = to leave. Separately, 'embora' alone can also mean 'although' as a conjunction - a different, unrelated use.
cuidado: ⚠️ 'embora' no siempre significa 'aunque'; en la expresión 'ir embora' significa simplemente 'irse', sin relación con el sentido concesivo.
ear: nasal 'em-': sounds like [ẽj-BO-ra], not an open 'en-' like in Spanish

sent1: Ele tem um jeito engraçado de falar.
sent1_en: He has a funny way of talking.
sent2: Não tem jeito, vamos ter que esperar.
sent2_en: There's no way around it, we'll have to wait.
notes: Very common noun: way, manner, knack, or a fix/solution to something.
ear: 'j' sounds like English/French 'zh', not the Spanish jota: jeito -> [ˈʒejtu]

sent1: Ele sempre dá um jeitinho pra resolver tudo.
sent1_en: He always finds a little workaround to solve everything.
sent2: O jeitinho brasileiro nem sempre é legal.
sent2_en: The Brazilian 'jeitinho' isn't always a good thing.
notes: Diminutive of 'jeito'; refers to the cultural habit of finding informal workarounds ('jeitinho brasileiro').

sent1: Pode esquentar a comida no micro-ondas.
sent1_en: You can heat up the food in the microwave.
sent2: Relaxa, não esquenta com isso.
sent2_en: Relax, don't worry about it.
notes: 'não esquenta' is a fixed colloquial phrase for 'don't worry about it'.

gloss: to get in the way, hinder, mess up
sent1: Sai da frente, você tá atrapalhando.
sent1_en: Get out of the way, you're getting in the way.
sent2: A chuva atrapalhou todo o passeio.
sent2_en: The rain messed up the whole outing.
cuidado: ⚠️ No es 'atropellar' (arrollar con un vehículo); significa 'estorbar' o 'entorpecer'.

gloss: to mess up, make a mess of
sent1: As crianças bagunçaram o quarto inteiro.
sent1_en: The kids messed up the whole room.
sent2: Não bagunça meus papéis, por favor.
sent2_en: Don't mess up my papers, please.
notes: From the noun 'bagunça' (mess, chaos).

sent1: Ela adora fofoca do trabalho.
sent1_en: She loves gossip from work.
sent2: Não vim aqui pra fofoca, quero falar sério.
sent2_en: I didn't come here for gossip, I want to talk seriously.
notes: Verb form is 'fofocar' (to gossip).

sent1: Tô com saudade da minha família.
sent1_en: I'm missing my family.
sent2: Que saudade daquele tempo de escola!
sent2_en: I really miss those school days!
notes: Famously said to have no exact translation; blends missing, nostalgia, and affection into one word.

sent1: Meu avô é muito teimoso, não muda de ideia.
sent1_en: My grandfather is very stubborn, he doesn't change his mind.
sent2: Para de ser teimosa e escuta o médico.
sent2_en: Stop being stubborn and listen to the doctor.

sent1: Ninguém gosta de puxa-saco no trabalho.
sent1_en: Nobody likes a brown-noser at work.
sent2: Ele só elogia o chefe, é um puxa-saco.
sent2_en: He only praises the boss, he's such a kiss-up.
notes: Literally 'bag-puller'; a vivid, very informal compound noun used constantly in spoken register.
ear: 'x' sounds like 'sh': puxa -> [ˈpuʃa], not like an 's' or 'ks'

gloss: to annoy, be a pain, nag
sent1: Para de encher o saco, já disse que não!
sent1_en: Stop being annoying, I already said no!
sent2: Esse barulho tá enchendo o saco de todo mundo.
sent2_en: This noise is annoying everyone.
notes: Literally 'to fill the bag'; mildly vulgar idiom, safe in casual contexts, means to annoy or nag.

gloss: annoying person, pain in the neck
sent1: Ele é tão mala que ninguém quer sair com ele.
sent1_en: He's such a pain that nobody wants to hang out with him.
sent2: Que mala essa mulher, não para de reclamar.
sent2_en: What a pain that woman is, she doesn't stop complaining.
cuidado: ⚠️ No es 'maleta'; aquí 'mala' es un adjetivo coloquial para persona pesada/molesta (parecido en espíritu a 'malo', pero no es lo mismo).

gloss: to joke around, tease, mess around
sent1: Para de zoar comigo, isso não tem graça.
sent1_en: Stop teasing me, that's not funny.
sent2: A galera ficou zoando até tarde no churrasco.
sent2_en: The crew kept joking around late at the barbecue.
notes: Can mean both 'to tease/joke' and 'to mess something up', depending on context.

sent1: A gente curtiu muito a viagem pra praia.
sent1_en: We really enjoyed the trip to the beach.
sent2: Curti sua foto nova, ficou linda.
sent2_en: I liked your new photo, it turned out beautiful.
cuidado: ⚠️ No tiene relación con 'curtir cuero/piel' del español; en portugués significa 'disfrutar' o 'dar like'.

gloss: to agree, be up for it
sent1: Você topa ir no show comigo sábado?
sent1_en: Are you up for going to the show with me on Saturday?
sent2: Ele topou ajudar a gente na mudança.
sent2_en: He agreed to help us with the move.
cuidado: ⚠️ No significa 'toparse con' (encontrarse por casualidad); aquí es 'aceptar, estar dispuesto a algo'.

sent1: Eu não saquei nada do que ele explicou.
sent1_en: I didn't get anything he explained.
sent2: Você sacou a piada ou não?
sent2_en: Did you get the joke or not?
cuidado: ⚠️ No significa 'sacar/retirar' algo; en este uso coloquial significa 'entender, pillar' una idea.

gloss: to be screwed, in deep trouble
sent1: Se ele descobrir, tamo ferrado.
sent1_en: If he finds out, we're screwed.
sent2: Esqueci a senha e agora tô ferrado.
sent2_en: I forgot the password and now I'm screwed.
notes: 'Estar ferrado' figuratively means to be in trouble/screwed; not commonly recognizable from Spanish.
ear: strong guttural r: ferrado -> [feˈʁadu], the 'r' sounds close to Spanish jota

sent1: A gente ficou batendo papo até tarde da noite.
sent1_en: We stayed chatting until late at night.
sent2: Vamos bater um papo depois do trabalho?
sent2_en: Want to have a chat after work?
notes: 'Papo' alone also means 'chat/talk', as in 'qual é o papo?' (what's up?).

sent1: Ele veio só pra bater um papo comigo.
sent1_en: He came over just to chat with me.
sent2: Rolou um papo sério lá em casa ontem.
sent2_en: There was a serious talk at home yesterday.
notes: Used in 'bater papo' (to chat) and 'papo reto' (straight talk).
cuidado: ⚠️ NO tiene relación con 'papada' ni con el 'papo' (buche) de los animales en español. Significa charla/conversación.

sent1: Hoje eu tenho muito trampo pra fazer.
sent1_en: Today I have a lot of work to do.
sent2: Ela arrumou um trampo novo semana passada.
sent2_en: She got a new job last week.
notes: Very casual slang for 'trabalho', common among younger speakers.
cuidado: ⚠️ NO significa 'trampa' (engaño). Es la forma coloquial de 'trabalho': curro, chamba.

sent1: Vamos ali comprar um rango bem rápido.
sent1_en: Let's go grab some food real quick.
sent2: O rango daquele restaurante é sensacional.
sent2_en: The food at that restaurant is amazing.
notes: Very informal slang for 'comida'.
cuidado: ⚠️ NO es 'rango' (jerarquía) del español. Significa comida, manduca.

sent1: Bora tomar uma birita depois do trampo?
sent1_en: Want to grab a drink after work?
sent2: Ele trouxe uma birita gelada pra festa.
sent2_en: He brought a cold drink to the party.
notes: Informal slang for an alcoholic drink, especially beer or cachaça.

sent1: A gente ficou na resenha até tarde.
sent1_en: We stayed hanging out and talking until late.
sent2: Vai ter uma resenha boa no fim de semana.
sent2_en: There's going to be a good get-together this weekend.
notes: Not just a book/movie 'review' — a casual social gathering to talk and drink.
cuidado: ⚠️ NO es solo 'reseña' de libro o película. Es también una reunión informal para charlar y beber.

sent1: Eu não sabia a resposta, aí dei um chute.
sent1_en: I didn't know the answer, so I just guessed.
sent2: Se não souber, pode dar um chute mesmo.
sent2_en: If you don't know, you can just take a wild guess.
notes: 'Chute' literally means a kick; 'dar um chute' figuratively means to guess randomly.
cuidado: ⚠️ NO es 'chute' de droga ni un disparo. 'Dar um chute' significa adivinar a lo loco, tirar un palo.

sent1: Para de mexer com ela, isso não tem graça.
sent1_en: Stop messing with her, that's not funny.
sent2: Os meninos vivem mexendo com o irmão mais novo.
sent2_en: The boys are always messing with their younger brother.
notes: Literally 'to move/stir'; with 'com alguém' it means to tease or provoke someone.
cuidado: ⚠️ 'Mexer' solo es 'mover/remover', pero 'mexer com alguém' significa meterse con alguien, molestarlo (a veces en tono de coqueteo).

sent1: Ela me cutucou pra eu prestar atenção.
sent1_en: She poked me to get my attention.
sent2: Não fica cutucando o cachorro, ele pode morder.
sent2_en: Don't keep poking the dog, he might bite.
notes: Physical poke with a finger or elbow, often to get attention.

sent1: O vento forte derrubou a árvore do quintal.
sent1_en: The strong wind knocked down the tree in the yard.
sent2: Cuidado pra não derrubar o copo da mesa.
sent2_en: Be careful not to knock the glass off the table.

sent1: Pode jogar fora essa caixa velha, por favor.
sent1_en: You can throw away that old box, please.
sent2: Ela jogou fora as roupas que não usava mais.
sent2_en: She threw away the clothes she wasn't wearing anymore.
notes: 'Jogar' alone means 'to play' or 'to throw'; 'jogar fora' specifically means to discard.
cuidado: ⚠️ NO es solo 'jugar'. 'Jogar fora' significa tirar algo a la basura, deshacerse de algo.
ear: j soa como 'j' francesa/inglesa, não como jota espanhola: jogar -> [ʒoˈgaɾ]

sent1: Preciso arrumar meu quarto antes de sair.
sent1_en: I need to tidy up my room before going out.
sent2: Ele conseguiu arrumar um trampo novo rapidinho.
sent2_en: He managed to land a new job pretty quickly.
notes: Very versatile verb: arrumar a casa (tidy the house), arrumar namorado (get a boyfriend), arrumar emprego (land a job).

sent1: O mecânico vai consertar meu carro amanhã.
sent1_en: The mechanic is going to fix my car tomorrow.
sent2: Preciso consertar essa torneira que tá vazando.
sent2_en: I need to fix this leaking faucet.
cuidado: ⚠️ NO es 'concertar' (acordar una cita). 'Consertar' significa reparar/arreglar algo roto.

sent1: E aí, beleza? Faz tempo que a gente não se vê.
sent1_en: Hey, what's up? It's been a while since we've seen each other.
sent2: Tudo beleza por aí, ou rolou algum perrengue?
sent2_en: Is everything cool over there, or was there some trouble?
notes: Used as greeting/confirmation: 'beleza?' (all good?), 'beleza!' (cool!, sounds good).
cuidado: ⚠️ Como interjección, 'beleza' NO se refiere a la belleza física. '¿Tudo beleza?' significa ¿todo bien?, vale/genial.

sent1: Aquele bicho lá é meio esquisito, hein.
sent1_en: That guy over there is kind of weird, you know.
sent2: Fala, bicho, quanto tempo que a gente não se via!
sent2_en: Hey man, it's been so long since we last saw each other!
notes: Informal way to refer to a person, like 'cara' or 'mano'.
cuidado: ⚠️ Además de 'animal/bicho', se usa informalmente para referirse a una persona: tío, tipo.

sent1: Aquele cara ali é meu vizinho novo.
sent1_en: That guy over there is my new neighbor.
sent2: Um cara estranho perguntou meu nome no ônibus.
sent2_en: A strange guy asked me my name on the bus.
notes: Extremely common word for 'guy/dude', not about the face here.
cuidado: ⚠️ NO es la 'cara' (rostro) ni la fachada de la casa aquí. Referido a una persona significa tío, tipo.

sent1: Isso que ele fez foi uma molecagem sem tamanho.
sent1_en: What he did was such a childish prank.
sent2: Para com essa molecagem e vem ajudar aqui.
sent2_en: Cut out the nonsense and come help over here.
notes: From 'moleque' (kid); refers to childish or silly behavior.

sent1: Fazer isso com ela foi uma sacanagem daquelas.
sent1_en: Doing that to her was a really dirty move.
sent2: Que sacanagem, ele nem avisou que ia cancelar.
sent2_en: What a jerk move, he didn't even warn us he'd cancel.
notes: Can also mean something naughty/sexual depending on context; here it's the 'unfair trick' sense.

sent1: Ele foi bem sacana ao esconder meu celular.
sent1_en: He was pretty mean hiding my phone.
sent2: Não seja sacana, devolve o dinheiro dela.
sent2_en: Don't be a jerk, give her money back.
notes: Adjective/noun for someone who acts unfairly or slyly.

gloss: to mess up, let someone down
sent1: Ele vacilou feio ao esquecer o aniversário dela.
sent1_en: He really messed up by forgetting her birthday.
sent2: Se você vacilar de novo, ela não perdoa.
sent2_en: If you screw up again, she won't forgive you.
notes: Not about 'hesitating' or 'joking' as in Spanish; it means to fail or behave badly.
cuidado: ⚠️ NO significa 'dudar' ni 'bromear' como en español. En portugués brasileño significa meter la pata, fallarle a alguien, portarse mal.

gloss: to pay attention to, show romantic interest
sent1: Ela nem deu bola pra ele na festa.
sent1_en: She didn't even give him the time of day at the party.
sent2: Eu tentei falar com ela, só que ela não deu bola nenhuma pra mim.
sent2_en: I tried to talk to her, but she didn't pay any attention to me.
notes: Used for romantic or social interest — showing (or ignoring) someone flirtatiously.

gloss: to hook up with, make out with (someone)
sent1: Eu fiquei com um cara ontem na balada.
sent1_en: I made out with a guy at the club last night.
sent2: Ela ficou com o amigo do meu irmão na festa de sábado.
sent2_en: She hooked up with my brother's friend at Saturday's party.
notes: Brazilian slang for a casual romantic/make-out encounter, unrelated to 'staying'.
cuidado: ⚠️ No es 'quedarse' ni 'quedar con alguien' (quedar con alguien = arrange to meet). 'Ficar com alguém' significa enrollarse/liarse con alguien.

sent1: Rolou uma festa lá em casa no fim de semana.
sent1_en: There was a party at my place over the weekend.
sent2: Não sei se vai rolar de eu ir no show hoje.
sent2_en: I don't know if I'll be able to make it to the show today.
notes: Colloquial for 'to happen' or 'to be possible', not just literal 'to roll'.
cuidado: ⚠️ No es solo 'rodar'. 'Rolar' también significa pasar/ocurrir algo, o ser posible.

gloss: to stall, string along with excuses
sent1: Ele ficou me embromando e nunca pagou o que devia.
sent1_en: He kept stringing me along and never paid what he owed.
sent2: Para de embromar e me diz logo se cê vem ou não.
sent2_en: Stop stalling and just tell me if you're coming or not.
notes: To delay someone with excuses or mild deception.
cuidado: ⚠️ En español rioplatense 'embromar' es fastidiar/arruinar. En portugués brasileño significa dar largas o entretener con excusas.

gloss: to stall, waffle, drag one's feet
sent1: Ele enrolou um monte antes de responder minha pergunta.
sent1_en: He beat around the bush a lot before answering my question.
sent2: Não enrola, fala logo o que cê quer.
sent2_en: Don't stall, just say what you want.
notes: Also used reflexively 'se enrolar', meaning to get confused or tangled up.
cuidado: ⚠️ No es 'enrollar' (papel). 'Enrolar' significa dar largas, enredar con palabras, hacer perder tiempo.

sent1: Aquele malandro sempre dá um jeito de não pagar a conta.
sent1_en: That sly guy always finds a way to avoid paying the bill.
sent2: Cuidado com ele, é malandro pra caramba.
sent2_en: Watch out for him, he's incredibly cunning.
notes: Can be playful admiration or a warning; a classic Brazilian archetype of streetwise cunning.

sent1: Você vem na festa, né?
sent1_en: You're coming to the party, right?
sent2: Tá calor hoje, né?
sent2_en: It's hot today, isn't it?
notes: Contraction of 'não é', used constantly as a confirmation tag at the end of sentences.

sent1: Aí eu cheguei em casa e vi que a porta tava aberta.
sent1_en: So then I got home and saw the door was open.
sent2: A gente foi pro shopping, aí resolveu ir ao cinema também.
sent2_en: We went to the mall, then decided to go to the movies too.
notes: Not locative 'there' — a filler linking one beat of narration to the next.
cuidado: ⚠️ No siempre significa 'ahí' (lugar). Muchas veces es solo un conector de continuación, tipo 'entonces, y bueno'.

sent1: Então, vamos começar a reunião.
sent1_en: So, let's start the meeting.
sent2: Então, eu acho melhor a gente deixar pra depois.
sent2_en: Well, I think it's better if we leave it for later.
notes: Often just opens or closes a turn, not strictly the causal 'therefore'.
cuidado: ⚠️ No siempre es 'entonces' causal. Muchas veces solo marca el inicio o cierre de un turno de habla, como 'bueno, pues'.

sent1: Ele é tipo muito estressado com o trabalho ultimamente.
sent1_en: He's like really stressed about work lately.
sent2: Eu queria fazer alguma coisa diferente, tipo viajar sozinha.
sent2_en: I wanted to do something different, like traveling alone.
notes: Ubiquitous filler for vagueness or giving an example, like English 'like'.

sent1: Eu fiquei, assim, sem saber o que responder.
sent1_en: I was just kind of at a loss for what to say.
sent2: Ela é, assim, muito família, sabe?
sent2_en: She's, like, very close to her family, you know?
notes: As a filler it doesn't mean 'like this/so' — it just hedges or softens a statement.
cuidado: ⚠️ No siempre significa 'así' (comparación). Como muletilla es solo relleno, tipo 'o sea, este...'.

sent1: Eu não tava afim de sair, sabe?
sent1_en: I just wasn't in the mood to go out, you know?
sent2: Ele é meio complicado, sabe, mas no fundo é gente boa.
sent2_en: He's kind of difficult, you know, but deep down he's a good person.
notes: Tag used to check the listener is following, like English 'you know'.

sent1: Pois é, a vida não é fácil mesmo.
sent1_en: Yeah, well, life really isn't easy.
sent2: Pois é, eu também queria ter ido, mas não deu.
sent2_en: Yeah, I wanted to go too, but it didn't work out.
notes: A resigned agreement or closing remark, like 'yeah, that's how it is'.

sent1: Ó, vem cá um instante, por favor.
sent1_en: Hey, come here a second, please.
sent2: Ó, esquece o que eu falei, tá?
sent2_en: Hey, forget what I said, okay?
notes: A very short, punchy interjection to grab attention, distinct from 'olha'.

gloss: look, hey (attention-getter, intro to a comment)
sent1: Olha, eu acho que você tá certo dessa vez.
sent1_en: Look, I think you're right this time.
sent2: Olha só que interessante essa história.
sent2_en: Look how interesting this story is.
notes: Literally the imperative of 'olhar' (to look), but used constantly as a discourse marker.

sent1: Cara, essa prova foi difícil demais.
sent1_en: Man, that test was way too hard.
sent2: Vamo lá, cara, a gente já tá atrasado.
sent2_en: Come on, dude, we're already late.
notes: Extremely common informal vocative for 'dude/man', also an interjection of surprise or emphasis.
cuidado: ⚠️ Aunque también significa 'rostro', como vocativo no tiene ese sentido — equivale a 'tío/colega'.

sent1: Sei lá, acho que vou ficar em casa hoje.
sent1_en: I dunno, I think I'll just stay home today.
sent2: Por que ele fez isso? Sei lá, ele é doido mesmo.
sent2_en: Why did he do that? No idea, he's just crazy.
notes: A casual shrug of not knowing or not caring much, softer than 'não sei'.

sent1: Pô, cara, você podia ter avisado antes.
sent1_en: Man, you could've told me sooner.
sent2: Pô, que saco isso, hein.
sent2_en: Ugh, what a pain, huh.
notes: Mild interjection of frustration or emphasis, softer than a curse word, very common in urban Brazilian speech.

sent1: Poxa, eu queria muito ter ido nessa viagem.
sent1_en: Aw man, I really wanted to go on that trip.
sent2: Poxa, que pena que ela não pôde vir.
sent2_en: Darn, what a shame she couldn't come.
notes: Softer, more polite interjection than 'pô', expressing mild disappointment or surprise.

sent1: Nossa, que trânsito enorme hoje!
sent1_en: Wow, what huge traffic today!
sent2: Nossa, eu nem acreditei quando vi a notícia.
sent2_en: Wow, I couldn't even believe it when I saw the news.
notes: Short for 'Nossa Senhora' (Our Lady); a pure exclamation of surprise, unrelated to 'our' in everyday use.
cuidado: ⚠️ No significa 'nuestra' aquí. Es una interjección de sorpresa, tipo '¡madre mía!' o '¡vaya!'.

sent1: Eita, eu esqueci o guarda-chuva de novo.
sent1_en: Whoa, I forgot the umbrella again.
sent2: Eita, caiu tudo no chão!
sent2_en: Whoa, everything fell on the floor!
notes: Interjection for sudden surprise or a small mishap; extremely common in spoken BR.

gloss: huh, well (Minas Gerais interjection of surprise/obviousness)
sent1: Uai, você não sabia disso?
sent1_en: Huh, you didn't know that?
sent2: Uai, mas é claro que eu vou!
sent2_en: Well, of course I'm going!
notes: Regional interjection strongly associated with Minas Gerais; marks surprise or stating the obvious.
ear: one glide, not three sounds: uai -> [uaj]

sent1: Opa, cuidado com o degrau aí!
sent1_en: Whoa, watch out for that step!
sent2: Opa, tudo bem contigo?
sent2_en: Hey, how's it going?
notes: Casual interjection: surprise, apologizing for bumping someone, or a brusque hello.

gloss: alright, deal, see ya (closing a conversation/agreement)
sent1: Falou, te vejo amanhã então.
sent1_en: Alright, see you tomorrow then.
sent2: Falou, pode deixar que eu cuido disso.
sent2_en: Deal, I'll take care of that.
notes: Closes a conversation or confirms agreement; unrelated to its literal meaning 'spoke'.
cuidado: ⚠️ No es '(él/ella) habló' aquí. Es una muletilla de cierre, como 'vale' o 'hecho'.

gloss: get it?, got it? (slang comprehension check)
sent1: Você tem que apertar aqui primeiro, sacou?
sent1_en: You have to press here first, get it?
sent2: Ela sacou na hora que eu tava brincando.
sent2_en: She got it right away that I was joking.
notes: Slang for 'to understand', from 'sacar' (to pull out); means 'get it' here, not withdrawing money.
cuidado: ⚠️ No es 'sacar dinero' aquí. Es jerga para 'entender, pillar'.

sent1: Putz, eu esqueci a senha de novo.
sent1_en: Ugh, I forgot the password again.
sent2: Putz, hoje o trânsito tá um caos.
sent2_en: Damn, traffic is chaos today.
notes: Mild euphemistic interjection of frustration, softer than a real curse word.

sent1: Ué, cadê o resto do bolo?
sent1_en: Huh, where's the rest of the cake?
sent2: Ué, você não ia sair hoje?
sent2_en: Huh, weren't you going out today?
notes: Expresses mild confusion or surprise, often when something doesn't match expectations.

gloss: oh okay, oh I see (sudden realization/acknowledgment)
sent1: Ah tá, agora eu entendi o que você quis dizer.
sent1_en: Oh I see, now I understand what you meant.
sent2: Ah tá, então foi por isso que ele não veio.
sent2_en: Oh okay, so that's why he didn't come.
notes: Signals sudden understanding or acknowledgment of new information.

gloss: huh?, what? (asking for repetition or seeking confirmation)
sent1: Hein? Pode repetir, eu não entendi.
sent1_en: Huh? Can you repeat that, I didn't understand.
sent2: Você vai mesmo, hein?
sent2_en: You're really going, huh?
notes: Filler for asking someone to repeat, or a sentence-final tag seeking confirmation.

gloss: okay?, you hear? (sentence-final tag for emphasis/confirmation)
sent1: Chega cedo amanhã, viu?
sent1_en: Get here early tomorrow, okay?
sent2: Não esquece de me ligar, viu?
sent2_en: Don't forget to call me, you hear?
notes: Sentence-final tag for emphasis or seeking agreement, unrelated to the verb 'ver' (to see).
cuidado: ⚠️ No es '(tú) viste' aquí. Es una coletilla de énfasis al final de la frase.

sent1: Tudo bem, a gente resolve isso depois.
sent1_en: Alright, we'll sort that out later.
sent2: Oi, tudo bem? Faz tempo que a gente não se vê.
sent2_en: Hey, how's it going? It's been a while since we've seen each other.
notes: Functions both as a greeting question and as an agreement/acceptance phrase.
cuidado: ⚠️ No es solo 'todo bien' como afirmación; también funciona como saludo, '¿qué tal?'.

gloss: it's just that, the thing is (introduces a justification)
sent1: Eu não fui, é que eu tava muito cansado.
sent1_en: I didn't go, it's just that I was really tired.
sent2: Ela chegou atrasada, é que o ônibus quebrou.
sent2_en: She arrived late, the thing is the bus broke down.
notes: Introduces an explanation or excuse, softer than a bare 'porque'.

gloss: in that case, actually, specifically (clarifying qualifier)
sent1: No caso, eu prefiro ir de manhã.
sent1_en: In that case, I'd rather go in the morning.
sent2: No caso, foi ela quem esqueceu a chave.
sent2_en: Actually, it was she who forgot the key.
notes: Used to specify or clarify a previous statement, similar to 'actually' or 'specifically'.
cuidado: ⚠️ No siempre equivale a 'en el caso de'; suele usarse para precisar o matizar lo dicho.

gloss: I mean, that is to say (reformulator)
sent1: Ele vem amanhã, quer dizer, depois de amanhã.
sent1_en: He's coming tomorrow, I mean, the day after tomorrow.
sent2: A gente vai atrasar, quer dizer, se o trânsito continuar assim.
sent2_en: We're going to be late, that is, if traffic keeps up like this.
notes: Reformulates or corrects what was just said, mid-sentence.

sent1: Ele chegou depois das dez, ou seja, bem atrasado.
sent1_en: He arrived after ten, in other words, quite late.
sent2: Ninguém confirmou, ou seja, ninguém vai vir.
sent2_en: Nobody confirmed, that is, nobody's coming.
notes: Introduces a restatement or clarifying conclusion.

sent1: Eu fiquei, tipo assim, sem saber o que falar.
sent1_en: I was, like, at a loss for words.
sent2: A festa foi, tipo assim, meio estranha.
sent2_en: The party was, kind of, a bit weird.
notes: Filler expressing vagueness or hesitation, common in casual youth speech.

gloss: what's up?, so then (greeting or topic transition)
sent1: E aí, beleza? Faz tempo que a gente não se fala.
sent1_en: What's up, all good? It's been a while since we talked.
sent2: E aí, o que você decidiu sobre a viagem?
sent2_en: So, what did you decide about the trip?
notes: Common casual greeting or discourse marker to pick up a topic.
cuidado: ⚠️ No significa 'y allí'; es un saludo casual o un conector para retomar el tema.

sent1: Pode deixar o prato aí, de boa.
sent1_en: You can leave the plate there, no worries.
sent2: Ele ficou de boa mesmo perdendo o jogo.
sent2_en: He stayed chill even after losing the game.
notes: Informal expression for being relaxed or okay with something.

sent1: Meu Deus, que susto você me deu!
sent1_en: Oh my God, you scared me!
sent2: Meu Deus, olha que trânsito é esse.
sent2_en: Good grief, look at this traffic.
notes: Common interjection of surprise, shock, or exasperation.

gloss: uh-oh, oh no (Northeastern interjection of alarm/surprise)
sent1: Vixe, acho que eu deixei o forno ligado.
sent1_en: Uh-oh, I think I left the oven on.
sent2: Vixe, ela caiu com tudo ali.
sent2_en: Oh no, she fell hard right there.
notes: Interjection of alarm or surprise, originally Northeastern Brazilian, now widely used.

gloss: don't even try, no way (flat rejection)
sent1: Nem vem com desculpa, cê sabia da hora.
sent1_en: Don't even try with excuses, you knew what time it was.
sent2: Nem vem que eu não vou emprestar o carro de novo.
sent2_en: Don't even try, I'm not lending you the car again.
notes: Shuts down a request or excuse before it's even finished; flat, informal rejection.

sent1: Que isso, imagina, não precisava trazer nada.
sent1_en: Oh come on, really, you didn't need to bring anything.
sent2: Desculpa o atraso! Que isso, nem percebi.
sent2_en: Sorry for being late! Don't worry about it, I didn't even notice.
notes: Polite formula to deflect thanks or an apology, minimizing the fuss.
cuidado: ⚠️ No significa '¿qué es esto?'. Es una fórmula de cortesía para restar importancia a un agradecimiento o disculpa.

sent1: Imagina, não foi incômodo nenhum te ajudar.
sent1_en: Don't mention it, it was no trouble at all helping you.
sent2: Vou te pagar amanhã, prometo. Imagina, pode ser quando quiser.
sent2_en: I'll pay you back tomorrow, I promise. No worries, whenever you want.
notes: Polite deflection used to dismiss thanks, apologies, or worry.
cuidado: ⚠️ No es 'imagínate' literal; es una forma cortés de decir 'de nada' o 'no te preocupes'.

sent1: Magina, cê não tá atrapalhando nada.
sent1_en: No way, you're not bothering anyone at all.
sent2: Ah desculpa se eu incomodei. Magina, fica tranquilo.
sent2_en: Oh sorry if I bothered you. Don't worry, relax.
notes: Very casual spoken reduction of 'imagina'; same meaning and use.
ear: imagina -> magina: initial unstressed i- drops in fast speech

sent1: Fala sério, ele ganhou na loteria de novo?
sent1_en: No way, he won the lottery again?
sent2: Ela terminou com o namorado por mensagem. Fala sério, que absurdo.
sent2_en: She broke up with her boyfriend over text. Seriously, how absurd.
notes: Expresses disbelief or emphasis; not a literal command to 'speak seriously'.
cuidado: ⚠️ No es una orden literal de 'habla en serio'; expresa incredulidad o énfasis.

sent1: Cê já viu aquele filme novo que todo mundo comenta?
sent1_en: Have you already seen that new movie everyone's talking about?
sent2: Cê vai no aniversário da Bia esse sábado?
sent2_en: Are you going to Bia's birthday this Saturday?
notes: Extremely common spoken reduction of 'você', used constantly in casual speech.
ear: você -> cê: first syllable drops entirely

sent1: Cês vão pra praia esse fim de semana?
sent1_en: Are you all going to the beach this weekend?
sent2: Cês já comeram? Fiz comida pra todo mundo.
sent2_en: Have you all eaten already? I made food for everyone.
notes: Plural spoken reduction of 'vocês', used when addressing a group.
ear: vocês -> cês: first syllable drops entirely

sent1: Tá chovendo muito lá fora, leva um guarda-chuva.
sent1_en: It's raining a lot outside, take an umbrella.
sent2: Ela tá com raiva de mim, mas não sei por quê.
sent2_en: She's angry with me, but I don't know why.
notes: Universal spoken reduction of 'está'; used everywhere in fast speech.

sent1: Tô com muita fome, vamo comer logo.
sent1_en: I'm really hungry, let's eat soon.
sent2: Não vou poder ir na festa, tô meio doente hoje.
sent2_en: I won't be able to go to the party, I'm a bit sick today.
notes: Spoken reduction of 'estou', extremely frequent in everyday speech.
ear: estou -> tô: reduces to a single syllable

sent1: Eu tava dormindo quando você ligou.
sent1_en: I was sleeping when you called.
sent2: Ela tava tão nervosa antes da prova.
sent2_en: She was so nervous before the exam.
notes: Spoken reduction of 'estava', very common in past-tense narration.

sent1: Eles tavam esperando cê chegar.
sent1_en: They were waiting for you to arrive.
sent2: As crianças tavam brincando no quintal a tarde toda.
sent2_en: The kids were playing in the backyard all afternoon.
notes: Spoken reduction of 'estavam', plural of 'tava'.

sent1: Eles tão numa reunião, liga depois.
sent1_en: They're in a meeting, call back later.
sent2: As lojas tão cheias hoje por causa da promoção.
sent2_en: The stores are packed today because of the sale.
notes: Spoken reduction of 'estão', plural verb form.
cuidado: ⚠️ No es el 'tan' español (tan bueno, tan grande). Es la forma reducida de 'estão' (ellos están).

sent1: Eu num sei se vou poder ir na festa amanhã.
sent1_en: I don't know if I'll be able to go to the party tomorrow.
sent2: Ele mora num apartamento pequeno perto do centro.
sent2_en: He lives in a small apartment near downtown.
notes: Two meanings depending on context: reduced spoken 'não' before a verb ('num sei' = 'não sei'), or the contraction of 'em' + 'um' meaning 'in a'.
cuidado: ⚠️ Cuidado: 'num' aquí no es una preposición española; es la reducción de 'não' o la contracción de 'em um', según el contexto.
ear: não -> num before a verb: nasalizes into a single syllable

sent1: Ela mora numa casa bem simples com os pais.
sent1_en: She lives in a very simple house with her parents.
sent2: A gente se conheceu numa festa de aniversário.
sent2_en: We met at a birthday party.
notes: Contraction of 'em' + 'uma', feminine counterpart of 'num'.

sent1: Vou comprar um presente pra minha irmã.
sent1_en: I'm going to buy a gift for my sister.
sent2: A gente precisa sair cedo pra pegar o ônibus.
sent2_en: We need to leave early to catch the bus.
notes: Universal spoken reduction of 'para', used across almost every register.

sent1: Vou levar as crianças pro parque hoje à tarde.
sent1_en: I'm going to take the kids to the park this afternoon.
sent2: Ele mandou o relatório pro chefe antes do prazo.
sent2_en: He sent the report to the boss before the deadline.
notes: Contraction of 'para' + 'o', masculine counterpart of 'pra'.

sent1: Vamo logo, o filme já vai começar.
sent1_en: Let's go quickly, the movie's about to start.
sent2: Vamo lá ver o que aconteceu com ela.
sent2_en: Let's go see what happened with her.
notes: Casual spoken reduction of 'vamos', dropping the final -s.

sent1: Bora, já tá tarde e a gente precisa sair.
sent1_en: Come on, it's already late and we need to leave.
sent2: Ninguém quer ficar em casa hoje. Bora pro shopping?
sent2_en: Nobody wants to stay home today. Let's go to the mall?
notes: Very common invitation marker, shortened from 'embora', used to prompt action.

sent1: Tá bom, eu vou fazer do jeito que você quer.
sent1_en: Okay, I'll do it the way you want.
sent2: Podemos remarcar pra sexta? Tá bom, sem problema.
sent2_en: Can we reschedule to Friday? Okay, no problem.
notes: General agreement marker meaning 'okay' or 'alright', very frequent in conversation.

sent1: Deixa eu ver isso aqui rapidinho.
sent1_en: Let me see this real quick.
sent2: Deixa eu te contar uma coisa engraçada.
sent2_en: Let me tell you something funny.
notes: Common conversational opener; the formal 'deixe-me' is almost never said aloud.
ear: fast speech collapses this to 'deixeu' or just 'xeu'

sent1: Ele tava falano sozinho no quarto.
sent1_en: He was talking to himself in the room.
sent2: Para de falano alto, o bebê tá dormindo.
sent2_en: Stop talking loudly, the baby is sleeping.
notes: Gerund -ndo commonly reduces to -no in casual speech.

sent1: Cê tá fazeno o quê aí?
sent1_en: What are you doing there?
sent2: Ela tava fazeno bagunça na cozinha.
sent2_en: She was making a mess in the kitchen.
notes: Same -ndo -> -no pattern as falano, comeno.

sent1: Ela tá comeno pipoca no sofá.
sent1_en: She's eating popcorn on the couch.
sent2: As crianças tavam comeno tudo da mesa.
sent2_en: The kids were eating everything on the table.
notes: Same -ndo -> -no pattern as falano, fazeno.

sent1: Peraí que eu já tô indo!
sent1_en: Hold on, I'm already on my way!
sent2: Ele tava indo pro trabalho quando viu o acidente.
sent2_en: He was going to work when he saw the accident.
notes: Standard gerund of 'ir'; often heard glued together inside 'tô indo'.

sent1: Eu preciso falá com você agora.
sent1_en: I need to talk to you now.
sent2: Ninguém quer falá sobre isso hoje.
sent2_en: Nobody wants to talk about this today.
notes: Popular speech drops the infinitive's final -r.

sent1: Bora comê antes que esfria.
sent1_en: Let's eat before it gets cold.
sent2: Não deu tempo de comê direito hoje.
sent2_en: There wasn't time to eat properly today.
notes: Same final -r drop as falá.

sent1: Eu preciso dormi cedo hoje.
sent1_en: I need to sleep early today.
sent2: Ele foi dormi depois da novela.
sent2_en: He went to sleep after the soap opera.
notes: Infinitive with dropped final -r; context (preciso, foi + verb) signals infinitive, not past tense.
cuidado: OJO: no es el pretérito español 'dormí' (yo dormí). Aquí es el infinitivo 'dormir' sin la -r final.

sent1: Cê vai sair com a gente hoje à noite?
sent1_en: Are you going out with us tonight?
sent2: Cê vai ver que vai dar tudo certo.
sent2_en: You'll see everything will work out.
notes: 'Cê' is the everyday reduction of 'você' before verbs.

sent1: Cê tá bem? Parece cansado.
sent1_en: Are you okay? You look tired.
sent2: Cê tá enganado, não foi assim.
sent2_en: You're mistaken, it wasn't like that.
notes: Same 'cê' reduction as cê vai, paired with 'tá' for 'está'.

gloss: I'm on my way, I'm coming
sent1: Espera aí, tô indo!
sent1_en: Wait up, I'm coming!
sent2: Fala pra ela que eu já tô indo pra festa.
sent2_en: Tell her I'm already on my way to the party.
notes: Fixed chunk used to say you're on your way.

sent1: Eu avisei, tá vendo? Deu certo.
sent1_en: I told you, see? It worked.
sent2: Ela chegou atrasada de novo, tá vendo.
sent2_en: She showed up late again, see.
notes: Used as a confirmation filler ('see?', 'told you'), not literally asking if someone sees.

sent1: Ele guardou o dinheiro no bolso.
sent1_en: He put the money in his pocket.
sent2: A gente se encontra no shopping às seis.
sent2_en: We'll meet at the mall at six.
notes: Contraction of 'em' + 'o'.
cuidado: OJO: idéntico a 'no' de negación en español, pero aquí NO es negación — es la contracción de 'em' + 'o' (en el).

sent1: Ela guardou as chaves na bolsa.
sent1_en: She put the keys in her purse.
sent2: Ela deixou o guarda-chuva na entrada de casa.
sent2_en: She left the umbrella at the entrance of the house.
notes: Contraction of 'em' + 'a'.
cuidado: OJO: no es el 'na' coloquial español (nada) — aquí es la contracción de 'em' + 'a' (en la).

sent1: Guardamos as fotos nos álbuns antigos.
sent1_en: We kept the photos in the old albums.
sent2: Eles moram nos Estados Unidos com a família.
sent2_en: They live in the United States with their family.
notes: Contraction of 'em' + 'os'; sounds identical to the pronoun 'nos' but means something different.
cuidado: OJO: aquí es la contracción de 'em' + 'os' (en los), no el pronombre 'nos' (nosotros/nos).

sent1: As crianças brincam nas férias de julho.
sent1_en: The kids play during the July vacation.
sent2: Ela guarda os documentos nas gavetas da mesa.
sent2_en: She keeps the documents in the desk drawers.
notes: Contraction of 'em' + 'as'.

sent1: Isso é do meu irmão, não mexe.
sent1_en: That's my brother's, don't touch it.
sent2: Ela chegou perto do fim da fila.
sent2_en: She arrived near the end of the line.
notes: Contraction of 'de' + 'o'; extremely high-frequency word.

sent1: Ela é da cidade do Rio de Janeiro.
sent1_en: She's from the city of Rio de Janeiro.
sent2: Isso saiu direto da boca dele.
sent2_en: That came straight out of his mouth.
notes: Contraction of 'de' + 'a'.
cuidado: OJO: idéntico a 'da' (él/ella da, del verbo dar) en español, pero aquí NO es del verbo dar — es la contracción de 'de' + 'a' (de la).

sent1: A gente foi pela praia até o final da tarde.
sent1_en: We walked along the beach until late afternoon.
sent2: Ele passou pela rua errada e se perdeu.
sent2_en: He went down the wrong street and got lost.
notes: Contraction of 'por' + 'a'.
cuidado: OJO: coincide con la forma verbal española 'pela' (de pelar), pero aquí es la contracción de 'por' + 'a' (por la).

sent1: Esse carro é dele, não é meu.
sent1_en: That car is his, not mine.
sent2: Peguei emprestado o livro dele semana passada.
sent2_en: I borrowed his book last week.
notes: Contraction of 'de' + 'ele'.

sent1: Essa bolsa é dela, não é minha.
sent1_en: This bag is hers, it's not mine.
sent2: Ele pegou o celular dela sem pedir.
sent2_en: He took her phone without asking.
notes: Contraction of 'de' + 'ela'; the mandatory spoken form, never 'de ela'.

sent1: Eu confio muito nele.
sent1_en: I trust him a lot.
sent2: Bota a chave nele antes de sair.
sent2_en: Put the key in it before you leave.
notes: Contraction of 'em' + 'ele'.

sent1: Eu confiei nela desde o início.
sent1_en: I trusted her from the start.
sent2: Ele encostou na parede e ficou apoiado nela.
sent2_en: He leaned against the wall and stayed resting on it.
notes: Contraction of 'em' + 'ela'.

sent1: Eu gosto desse filme.
sent1_en: I like that movie.
sent2: Ninguém falou desse assunto na reunião.
sent2_en: Nobody talked about that subject in the meeting.
notes: Contraction of 'de' + 'esse'; refers to something near the listener.

sent1: Não mexe nesse computador.
sent1_en: Don't touch that computer.
sent2: Fica ligado nesse detalhe, é importante.
sent2_en: Stay tuned to that detail, it's important.
notes: Contraction of 'em' + 'esse'.

gloss: of that (over there), from that one
sent1: Gostei muito daquele restaurante.
sent1_en: I really liked that restaurant (over there).
sent2: Ninguém lembra mais daquele dia.
sent2_en: Nobody remembers that day anymore.
notes: Contraction of 'de' + 'aquele'; refers to something far from both speakers.

sent1: A gente se conheceu naquele show.
sent1_en: We met at that concert.
sent2: Ele ainda mora naquele bairro.
sent2_en: He still lives in that neighborhood.
notes: Contraction of 'em' + 'aquele'.

sent1: Não gosto disso.
sent1_en: I don't like that.
sent2: Cansei de falar disso com você.
sent2_en: I'm tired of talking about that with you.
notes: Contraction of 'de' + 'isso', neuter demonstrative (no gender, refers to an idea/situation).

sent1: Eu nem tinha pensado nisso.
sent1_en: I hadn't even thought about that.
sent2: Ela acredita muito nisso, viu.
sent2_en: She really believes in that, you know.
notes: Contraction of 'em' + 'isso'.

sent1: Cê quer ir no cinema comigo?
sent1_en: Do you want to go to the movies with me?
sent2: Fala logo, cê quer suco ou refri?
sent2_en: Speak up, do you want juice or soda?
notes: 'Cê' is the everyday spoken reduction of 'você', losing its first syllable; extremely common before a verb.
ear: você -> cê: perde a sílaba inicial 'vo-'

sent1: É isso memo, cara.
sent1_en: That's exactly it, dude.
sent2: Ela chegou atrasada memo, que absurdo.
sent2_en: She really did arrive late, how absurd.
notes: Colloquial pronunciation of 'mesmo', used as an intensifier meaning 'really/indeed'.
cuidado: ⚠️ 'Memo' en español significa 'tonto/bobo'. Aquí no tiene nada que ver: es la forma hablada de 'mesmo' (mismo), usada como intensificador.

sent1: Não sei o qui fazer agora.
sent1_en: I don't know what to do now.
sent2: Fala logo o qui você quer.
sent2_en: Just say what you want already.
notes: Relaxed spoken pronunciation of 'que' in fast speech; the final vowel raises to 'i'.
ear: que -> qui: a vogal final fecha para 'i' na fala rápida

sent1: Cabô a novela, agora vamo dormir.
sent1_en: The soap opera's over, let's go to sleep now.
sent2: Corre que cabô o tempo!
sent2_en: Hurry up, time's up!
notes: Colloquial spoken reduction of 'acabou' (it ended/finished), drops the initial 'a' and closes the final diphthong.
ear: acabou -> cabô: perde o 'a' inicial e o ditongo final fecha
